xh1 TransactionSynchronizatioRegistry (TSR)


TSR je poput HashMap-e koja je povezana sa transakcijom.

Koristimo ga tako sto u njega stavimo neke podatke i kada god smo u sinhronoj transakciji (sinhrono znaci da posaljemo request i cekamo dok ne dobijemo response) mozemo pristupati podacima. Ovo ne vazi za asinhrone transakcije jer se tada podaci gube.

xh1 Interfaces


Ne smatraju se pod best practice u Java EE-u. Dovoljni su public metodi EJB-ova ili CDI-ova i oni zapravo cine interface. Dobri su kada imamo razlicite implementacije istog algoritma i koristimo strategy pattern. Interface je robustan ako isporucuje kvalitetan JSON, npr., a prihvata bilo kakav JSON ali iz njega izvlaci samo ono sto mu treba.

xh1 Novac


Za rad sa novcem koristimo JSR (Java Specification Request) 354 - Money and Currency API.

xh1 Best practice


  1. Pisati prost kod i time omoguciti JVM-u da prvo "razume" kod a onda i da interno izvrsi optimizaciju koda. Ne optimizovati bez prethodno uocenih problema.

xh1 Redosled inicijalizacije objekta


  1. Ako postoji super klasa, prvo se ona inicijalizuje.
  2. static variable declaration i static initializers redosledom kojim se nalaze u file-u. A static block (initializer) is executed when the class that contains it is loaded. The idea is that any given class is loaded at most once during a Java program's execution. Therefore, static blocks are executed at most once. A class gets loaded the first time it is used. That generally means building an instance of it by calling a constructor, or accessing one of its static fields or methods. Other ways are for example, getting a Class introspection object that represents this class.
  3. instance variable declaration i instance initializers redosledom kojim se nalaze u file-u.
  4. konstruktor.

OCA 1Z0-808, 202. str.

static initializer-e koristimo kada inicijalizacija statickog dela klase zahteva vise linija koda.

Instance initializers se koriste kada zelimo da se odredjeni kod izvrsi pre izvrsavanja konstruktora. Ono sto se u pozadini desava je da se kod iz instance initializer-a kopira na pocetak svakog konstruktora klase. Na ovaj nacin mozemo definisati kod koji je zajednicki za sve konstruktore odredjene klase. Ako imamo 2 konstruktora, pri cemu 1 konstruktor poziva 2. sa this( … ), then compiler does not copy the instance initializer code into that constructor. <- CAKA.


Razlog zasto u klasi treba da imamo no-args konstruktor:

Kompajler ne dodaje no-args konstruktor u User klasu jer smo mi definisali jedan konstruktor, medjutim, u klasi Staff, kompajler pokusava da ubaci super() method, ali ne moze jer ne postoji no-args konstruktor u User klasi. Zato mi moramo eksplicitno definisati no-args konstruktor ili dodati super(id) u Staff konstruktor.


In general, it is a good idea to always include explicit superclass calls in your constructors (tj. super();), even if it is one that the compiler could generate automatically. We consider this good style, because it avoids the possibility of misinterpretation and confusion in case a reader is not aware of the automatic code generation.

xh1 Paketska struktura u projektu


Package name je deo klase i ne moze se menjati a da se ne uradi i rekompajliranje klase. File-ovi koji pripadaju istom paketu mogu biti u razlicitim folderima. U tom slucaju, package name je isti za svaku klasu u paketu ali putanje do klasa mogu pocinjati u razlicitim folderima. Akcenat je na delu "pocinjati u razlicitim folderima", odnosno klase mogu pripadati paketu a.b i biti u dir1, dir2 ili dir3 ali u okviru ovih foldera ce se nalaziti a/b folderska struktura. Primer ovoga su testovi u Maven Java Archetype folderskoj strukturi gde testovi pripadaju istom paketu kao i klase koje se testiraju ali se nalaze u razlicitim folderima:

src/main/java/com/companyName/graphics

src/test/java/com/companyName/graphics

Na ovaj nacin se source logika razdvaja, a sa 2. strane i dalje mozemo pristupati package private (default) metodama i poljima klase koju testiramo.

xh1 Filter


Koriste se u web aplikacijama zarad izvrsavanja odredjenih akcija nad request-om/response-om, pre nego dodje do ili nakon sto ode od, akcije koja se izvrsava na serveru.

xh1 General



James Gosling je tvorac Java-e.

Java je platform independent jezik sto znaci da 1 napisan kod mozemo izvrsavati bilo gde. Ovo je iskazano kroz moto Java-e: Write Once, Run Anywhere (WORA). Sa 2. strane C je platform dependent sto znaci da se kod kompajliran na Windows-u ne moze izvrsavati na Linux OS-u. Moramo kompajlirati source code na Linux-u da bi mogli da ga izvrsavamo na Linux OS-u, sto nije slucaj sa Java-om: kod mozemo kompajlirati na bilo kom OS-u i izvrsavati ga na nekom 2. OS-u. Kada se kaze platform dependent, ne misli se samo na OS, vec i na hardware, pa tako platform dependent jezici zavise i od arhitekture (tipa) procesora na kome se kompajliraju.

Much of Java’s success is due to the fact that it is highly portable across multiple platforms. The bytecodes produced by Java compiler can be executed on various platforms, as long as the system has a Java Virtual Machine loaded.

Pogledaj i stranicu "Hardware/Platform (in)dependency".


xh1 Osobine:

  1. Java je case-sensitive. <- VAZNO.
  2. Statically (strongly) typed jezik (tip promenljive se ne moze menjati nakon njene deklaracije). Static means that types are checked before run-time. On the other side are dynamic languages where types are checked on the fly, during execution. Druga osobina koja razlikuje jezike je to da li su compiled (code translated before run-time) ili interpreted (code translated on the fly, during execution). Pa je tako Java static i compiled, kao i Go npr. dok je Python dynamic and interpreted, kao i JavaScript.
  3. Instance i class (static) variable dobijaju default-ne vrednosti dok lokalne variable ne dobijaju default-ne vrednosti.

xh1 Terminologija

xh2 Paket (package)

One of the features of object-oriented languages that makes them powerful is that they are often accompanied by class libraries (biblioteke klasa). These libraries typically contain many hundreds or thousands of different classes that have proved useful to developers on a wide range of different projects. Java calls its libraries packages. Packages can be nested (that is, packages can contain other packages).


Na paket treba gledati kao na skup klasa koje pripadaju istoj familiji.


Library classes are used in exactly the same way as we would use our own classes.


Naming conventions when package name has 2 or more words:

  • do not use hypen "-" (bad practice),
  • do not use capital letters inside package names (bad practice),
  • just concatenate strings like com.vujo.mypackage (prefered),
  • use underscore "_".

Legalizing package names:

Domain Name / Package Name

  • hyphenated-name.example.org / org.example.hyphenated_name
  • example.int / int_.example
  • 123name.example.com / com.example._123name

https://docs.oracle.com/javase/tutorial/java/package/namingpkgs.html

xh2 Access modifiers

  1. Package private (default) access modifier znaci da su member-i klase dostupni samo klasama (koje ne moraju da budu child klase) u okviru istog paketa.
  2. protected access modifier znaci da su member-i dostupni u okviru istog paketa (svim ostalim klasama u tom paketu, ne samo child klasama) ali takodje i van paketa, ali samo u slucaju da se protected member-ima pristupa UNUTAR same child klase, koja se nalazi u 2. paketu. Ovaj poslednji konkretno znaci da ako imamo sledecu strukturu:

    3. i ako je Bird:

    obrati paznju na deo koji se ne kompajlira, u narednom kodu:

    Lines 15 and 16 doesn't compile because, this time, a Bird reference is used. It is created on line 14 and since Bird is in a different package, this code isn't inheriting from Bird, so it doesn't get to use protected members.

    Even through Swan inherits from Bird, the variable reference isn't a Swan, the code just happens to be in the Swan class.


    Keep in mind that, all methods/fields, even private, are inherited, but access modifier determine to which of these members are accessible to the inherited class.



xh2 Objekat

Logicka celina koja reprezentuje nesto, npr. dokument, string, file, … Objekat nudi i mogucnost manipulacije onoga sto reprezentuje koristeci metode koje su prikladne za taj objekat.

Objects have state (the fields) and behaviour (the methods).

xh2 Simple class name

Class name without the package. Znaci simple class name je string koji se nalazi nakon keyword-a class u definiciji klase. A class name is an identifier—a series of characters consisting of:

  1. letters,
  2. digits,
  3. underscores (_) and
  4. dollar signs ($)

that does not begin with a digit and does not contain spaces. Identifier moze biti ime klase, promenljive ili metoda.

As of Java 9, you can no longer use an underscore (_) by itself as an identifier. Notice that you can still use underscores in variable names. The only thing you cannot do is using a single underscore as an identifier.


Java 7 je dodala mogucnost koriscenja underscore-a pri definisanju numerickih vrednosti:

int x = 1_000_000;

Ovim feature je uveden iz cisto estetskih razloga.


Ako klasa ima definisan paket u kome se nalazi, onda naziv paketa, nakon kompajliranja, postaje deo imena klase i takav naziv klase se naziva fully qualified class name (FQCN). Posto je, nakon kompajliranja, naziv klase zapravo:

naziv paketa + naziv klase

to je razlog zasto, kada pokrecemo klasu, moramo navesti i ime paketa jer je to sada deo imena klase. Ako je naziv klase com.mds.SuperClass.class, onda na CLASSPATH-u moramo imati definisanu, kao jednu od potencijalnih lokacija .class file-ova, i parent foldera com, da bi pri pokretanju ove klase, Java interpreter (JVM) mogao da nadje na toj lokaciji foldersku strukturu koja odgovara nazivu klase. <- OBJASNJENJE ZASTO MORAMO DA NAVEDEMO FQCN KADA POKRECEMO KLASU.

xh2 Literal

A literal is the source code representation of a fixed value; literals are represented directly in your code without requiring computation. As shown below, it's possible to assign a literal to a variable of a primitive type.


Naziv za row data:

int x = 10;

String name = "John";

long y = 10L

long y = 10l (malo slovo L)


A floating-point literal is of type float if it ends with the letter F or f; otherwise its type is double and it can optionally end with the letter D or d.

The 10 and "John" are just a values itself, called a literal values, because they stands alone without being stored in a variable.

xh2 Expression

Statements are made up of one or more expressions.

An expression is any reference to a variable or value, or a set of variable(s) and value(s) combined with operators. <- OSNOVE!

For example:

a = b * 2;

This statement has four expressions in it:

• 2 is a literal value expression.

• b is a variable expression, which means to retrieve its current value.

• b * 2 is an arithmetic expression, which means to do the multiplication.

• a = b * 2 is an assignment expression, which means to assign the result of the b * 2 expression to the variable a.


Statement sama po sebi ne vraca vrednost dok expression vraca. <- SUSTINA!

Npr. if/else petlja je statement sto znaci da if/else petlja radi nesto, ali sama po sebi ne vraca vrednost.

xh2 Compiler

Computers, internally, work with a binary representation of a machine code (bytecode), which looks quite different from Java. The compiler is program whose job is to translates the Java code into machine code (bytecode).

xh2 Stereotype

Term for an annotation that encapsulates other annotations. Npr. @Model je built-in stereotype. Mogu se koristiti da bi nad klasom definisali vise interceptor-a jednom anotacijom.

xh2 Enterprise application

Provide the business logic for an enterprise.

xh2 Business logic

Code that fulfills the purpose of the application.

xh2 Managed Beans

Lightweight container-managed objects (POJOs) with minimal requirements, support a small set of basic services, such as resource injection, lifecycle callbacks, and interceptors. Container-managed objects are:

  1. servlets,
  2. enterprise bean, or
  3. managed bean.

A Java EE component is a bean if the lifecycle of its instances may be managed by the container according to the lifecycle context model defined in the CDI specification.

xh2 Callback methods

User defined methods that are attached to some object's lifecycle events and are invoked automatically by container when these events occur.

xh2 Resource

Program object that provides connections to such systems as database servers and messaging systems.

Each resource object is identified by a unique, people-friendly name, called the JNDI name. For example, the JNDI name of the preconfigured JDBC resource for the Java DB database that is shipped with GlassFish Server is java:comp/DefaultDataSource. Applications then use annotations to inject the resources. You inject resources by using the @Resource annotation in an application.

xh2 Resource adapter

Software component that allows Java EE application components to access and interact with the underlying resource manager of the EIS. Because a resource adapter is specific to its resource manager, a different resource adapter typically exists for each type of database or enterprise information system (EIS).

xh2 Web module

Smallest deployable and usable unit of web resources. A web module contains web components and static web content files, such as images, which are called web resources. Web module corresponds to a web application.

xh2 Object state

The state of an object consists of the values of its instance variables.

Generalno govoreci vrednosti koje se nalaze u promenljivama predstavljaju state bilo to objekta, programa ili neceg drugog u cemu su te promenljive definisane.

xh2 Helper class / Utility class

U Java-i je treba koristiti "utility class" naziv za ovakve klase.


Klase sa reusable static metodama.


Nuzno zlo u Java-i, posto ne postoji nacin da se reuse-uju funkcije van klase.

Pri kreiranju utility klasa treba paziti na barem 2 stvari:

  • da li je u pitanju stvarno re-usable funkcionalnost, u suprotnom je treba pripojiti postojećoj klasi koja se bavi baš time i koristiti instance metode a ne static.
  • ne stavljati sve u jednu helper klasu, već praviti više helper klasi po separation of concerns i package principima. Ako je struktura organizovana na osnovu domena, onda videti da li klasa pripada jednom domenu i staviti je tu, ako ne prebaciti i shared folder koji se bavi svim i svačim.

xh2 Coarse-grained object

Contains more data than a fine-grained one.

xh2 Batch (serija, gomila) jobs

Tasks that can be executed without user interaction. These tasks are executed periodically or when resource usage is low, and they often process large amounts of information such as log files, database records, or images. Examples include billing, report generation, data format conversion, and image processing.

The batch framework consists of a job specification language based on XML, a set of batch annotations and interfaces for application classes that implement the business logic, a batch container that manages the execution of batch jobs, and supporting classes and interfaces to interact with the batch container.

xh2 Batch processing

Refers to running batch jobs on a computer system.

xh2 bin folder

Contains both the compiler and the launcher.

xh2 Serialize

This means to convert object state to a byte stream so that the byte stream can be reverted back into a copy of the object. Deserialization is the process of converting the serialized form of an object back into a copy of the object.

xh2 Compiling

Process of translating source code (the human-readable description of a program written by a programmer) into the native language of the computer’s processor. It is done with program called a compiler. Na ovaj nacin se build-uju programi.

xh2 Interoperability

Way to enable disparate (razlicit) software applications to work together.

xh2 Truncation

Casting a floating-point value to an integer value may introduce truncation errors (loss of the fractional part) into the result.

xh2 Local variable

It's declarations look similar to field declarations, but they never have private or public as part of them.

Local variables must be initialized before they are used in an expression—they are not given a default value.

xh2 Method signature

Combination of the method's name and the number, types and order of its parameters, but NOT its return type. <- OBRATI PAZNJU.

xh2 Overload methods

Methods of the same name can be declared in the same class, as long as they have different sets of parameters (determined by the number, types and order of the parameters).

xh2 Override methods (redefinition of methods)

Potpuno isti metodi sa razlicitim implementacijama. Pravila pri override-ovanju metoda:

Staticki metodi se ne mogu override-ovati, vec se samo mogu hide-ovati. Ovo ne znaci da child klasa ne moze definisati staticki metod koji ima isti signature kao staticki metod parent klase vec znaci da ce se uvek pobuditi staticki metod reference type-a jer kompajler radi early binding i definise u bytecode-u i signature ali i implementaciju metoda koji ce se koristiti tako da, kada JVM dodje do statickog metoda u kodu, on nema sta da razmislja vec samo izvrsi definisani staticki metod a to je zapravo staticki metod definisan u reference type-u. Ako zelimo da iz child objekta, koji hide-uje staticki metod parent objekta, pristupimo statickom metodu parent-a onda koristimo ParentClassName.staticMethodName(). Ne mozemo koristiti super keyword jer super predstavlja objekat a staticki metodi zahtevaju klasu.


Contrary to the rule for super() call in constructors, the super call in methods may occur anywhere within that method. It does not have to be the first statement.


Kao posledicu override-ovanja metoda imamo method polymorphism (polymorphic method dispatch): method calls in Java are polymorphic. The same method call may at different times invoke different methods, depending on the dynamic type (zavisno od objekta na koji promenljiva ukazuje) of the variable used to make that call.

xh2 Argumenti vs parametri

Argumenti se prosledjuju funkciji (imaju vrednost) dok su parametri promenljive koje se koriste u funkciji. To su zapravo 2 strane istog novcica.

xh2 Hiding fields

When you hide a variable, you define a variable with the same name as a variable in a parent class. Kada se hide-uje polje, nije bitan tip polja, bitno je samo da imena polja budu ista.

Reference type determines which particular value of hiden field is used. <- OBRATI PAZNJU.

Knjiga za 1Z0-808 exam, 257. str.

xh2 Pass-by-reference or pass-by-value

In Java all arguments are passed by value, always. A method call can pass two types of values to a method—copies of primitive values (e.g., values of type int and double) and copies of references to objects. U oba slucaja se vrsi pass-by-value.


Zbog nacina na koji se primitive i reference cuvaju u memoriji, kada se argumenti passed by value, u slucaju primitiva, ta value je vrednost primitive dok je u slucaju referenci ta value memorijska adresa objekta. Zato kada dodeljujemo primitivu 2. promenljivi, promena 2. promenljive nece uticati na vrednost u 1 jer ce 2. primitiva imati svoju kopiju vrednosti. Dok u slucaju referenci, posto ce 2. referenca dobiti kopiju memorijske adrese objekta, sve promene koje 2. referenca ucini nad objektom, tj. UNUTAR objekta, koji joj je dodeljen, ce biti vidljive i kroz 1. referencu jer obe reference imaju isti vrednost, tj. memorijsku adresu i ukazuju na isti objekat. <- OBRATI PAZNJU.


JS koristi isti princip kao i Java. JS primer:

Dodelom novog objekta bilo kojoj od 2 reference, koje su ukazivale na isti objekat, prestaje da postoji bilo kakva zavisnost ove 2 reference, kao sto je prikazano u 1. funkciji.

xh2 Inheritance

Conflict resolution strategies

Rules for how implementation inheritance work:

  1. Classes win over interfaces. If a class in the superclass chain has a declaration for the method (concrete or abstract), you're done, and defaults (from interface) are irrelevant.
  2. More specific interfaces win over less specific ones (where specificity means "subtyping"). A default from List wins over a default from Collection, regardless of where or how or how many times List and Collection enter the inheritance graph.
  3. If there is not a unique winner according to the above rules, concrete classes must disambiguate manually.

Ovo su pravila koja uvek treba da imamo na umu kada zakljucujemo koji metod ce se naslediti u odredjenom slucaju (kada klasa implementira interface sa npr. default-nim metodama i ekstenduje (apstraktnu) klasu). <- VAZNO.


Par napomena vezaih za default-ne metode u interface-u:

  • default-ni metodi ne mogu override-ovati metode iz Object klase.
  • kada se eksplicitno poziva default-ni metod iz interface-a, koristi se super keyword u obliku: InterfaceName.super.defaultMethodName();. Moze se pozivati samo interface koji klasa direktno implementira, ne i grandparent interface.

    • Primer koda Examples/defaultMethods u Test environment folderu.

  • ne mogu se koristiti final i synchronized keywords uz default-ne metode.

We can inherit static methods.


We call the declared type of the variable the static type, because it is declared in the source code—the static representation of the program.

We call the type of the object stored in a variable the dynamic type, because it depends on assignments at runtime—the dynamic behavior of the program.

Vehicle v1 = new Car();

The static type of v1 is Vehicle, the dynamic type of v1 is Car.

For type checking, the static type is used. The dynamic type is often only known at runtime, so the compiler has no other choice but to use the static type if it wants to do any checks at compile time.

xh2 Compile-time (compiletime) dependency

Compiletime dependencies are only the dependencies (other classes) which you use DIRECTLY in the class you're compiling.

Compiletime dependencies are dependencies that we need on our CLASSPATH in order to compile artifact.


Java doesn't actually link anything at compile time. It only verifies the syntax using the matching classes it finds in the CLASSPATH. It's not until runtime that everything gets put together and executed based on the CLASSPATH at that time.

xh2 Runtime dependency

Runtime dependencies covers both the DIRECT and INDIRECT dependencies of the class you're running.

Runtime dependencies are dependencies that we need on our CLASSPATH in order to run artifact.

Thus, runtime dependencies includes dependencies of dependencies and any reflection dependencies like classnames which you have in a String, but are used in Class#forName().￼

Runtime dependency is more related with the actual flow-of-control.

xh2 Polymorphism ("many shapes")

Property of an object to take on many different forms.

To put this more precisely, a Java object may be accessed using a reference with the same type as the object, a reference that is a superclass of the object, or a reference that defines an interface the object implements, either directly or through a superclass. So reference type and actual object type can be different. This is why variables holding object types in Java are polymorphic variable. Furthermore, a cast is not required if the object is being reassigned to a super type or interface of the object.

So, a variable can hold objects of its declared type or of any subtype of its declared type. This principle is known as substitution - we can substitute a subclass object where a superclass object is expected, because the subclass object is a special case of the superclass.

Superclass se cesto naziva i supertype ili base class dok se subclass cesto naziva subtype ili derived class.


Kompajler koristi reference type da bi odredio koji metodi se mogu pozivati nad objektom dok sa 2. strane JVM koristi object type da bi odlucio koji tacno metod da pozove. JVM ce uvek pozvati najspecificniju verziju metoda, pocevsi od object type-a i iduci ka parent objektima: <- OBRATI PAZNJU. MNOGO VAZNO.

Method binding (method lookup or method dispatch)

Rekli smo da kompajler koristi reference type da bi odredio koji metodi mogu da se pozovu nad odredjenom referencom, dok JVM koristi object type da bi odredio koju implementaciju tog metoda treba pozvati. Ovaj proces se zove method binding. Pored instance metoda, method binding se vrsi i za staticke metode, tj. za svaki metod. Definicija method binding-a je: povezivanje method call-a sa odgovarajucom deklaracijom metoda kao i sa odgovarajucom implementacijom te deklaracije. Method binding se deli na 2 dela:

  1. method signature binding i
  2. method implementation binding.

Method signature binding se sastoji od sledeceg:

  1. Kada kompajler naidje na method invocation statement, on proverava da li tip reference, nad kojom se metod poziva, sadrzi kompatibilan metod, tj. da li postoji metod koji ima iste ili kompatibilne parametre.
  2. Ako kompajler pronadje takav metod onda on upisuje signature tog metoda u bytecode. Ako ne nadje, onda dobijemo compiler error. Ova logika se primenjuje na sve metode (i instance i static). Znaci u svakom slucaju se signature metoda upisuje u bytecode tokom kompajliranja (malo redundantna recenica jer naravno da se upisuje tokom kompajliranja, ne postoji 2. trenutak kad bi nesto moglo da se upise u bytecode ako ne tokom kompajliranja).

Nakon ovoga treba doneti odluku koju konkretnu implementaciju metoda treba izvrsiti tokom runtime-a. Ovu odluku moze doneti ili compiler ili JVM zavisno od tipa metoda. Ako je u pitanju static metod onda odluku donosi kompajler na osnovu tipa reference dok u slucaju instance metoda odluku donosi JVM na osnovu tipa objekta. JVM polazi od objekta trazeci 1. metod koji zadovaljava signature iz bytecode-a. Posto se odluka o tome koji staticki metod ce se izvrsti donosi tokom kompajliranja ovo se zove compile-time ili early binding. Sa 2. strane, u slucaju instance metoda imamo runtime ili late binding (dynamic binding). Late binding je karakteristican za override-ovane metode.

U slucaju polja objekta, primenjuje se early binding na osnovu tipa reference.

Da bi videli koji metod je kompajler izabrao (set-ovao u bytecode), koristimo javap tool kojim citamo sadrzaj bytecode-a, tj. .class file-ova.

Obavezno pogledati: Obuka "Udemy/Java in depth/Section 8/115. ep." + "Method Binding" u Obuke/Java/Udemy/Java in depth/Dokumentacija + primer koda "Examples/methodBinding".

Ako imamo kod:

v1.display();

when this statement executes, the display() method is invoked in the following steps:

  1. The variable v1 is accessed.
  2. The object stored in that variable is found (following the reference).
  3. The class of the object is found (following the instance of reference).
  4. The implementation of the display method is found in the class and executed.

Zbog ove procedure JVM polazi od objekta trazeci 1. metod koji zadovoljava signature iz bytecode-a. Ova procedura se primenljuje uvek bez obzira da li su static type i dynamic type isti ili se koristi polimorfizam ili je pak metod override-ovan.

Ovo objasnjava zasto se u slucaju override-ovanog metoda izvrsava metod u child klasi cak iako je static type (tip reference) parent klasa.

xh2 Immutable classes

Those class, whose object can not be modified once created, it means any modification on immutable object will result in another immutable object. Rules to make a class immutable:

  1. State of immutable object can not be modified after construction, any modification should result in new immutable object.
  2. All fields of immutable class should be final. Napomena je da final polja ne dobijaju default-nu vrednost, odnosno moramo ih rucno inicijalizovati tokom deklaracije ili u instance ili static initializer-u ili u konstruktoru.
  3. Object must be properly constructed i.e. object reference must not leak during construction process.
  4. Object should be final in order to restrict sub-class for altering immutability of parent class (to prevent subclasses from overriding methods, which can compromise immutability).
  5. Ako neko polje immutable objekta ukazuje na mutable objekat i ako to polje treba da bude dostupno i izvan objekta onda, svaki put kada neko trazi vrednost tog polja, treba mu vratiti kopiju mutable objekta na koje polje ukazuje (to prevent the risk of malicious client modifying mutable reference after getting it - reference leak).

Whenever a value of a primitive type is used in a context that requires a wrapper type, the compiler automatically wraps the primitive-type value in an appropriate wrapper object. xh2 Program vs. process vs. thread

A program is an executable file containing a set of instructions and passively stored on disk. One program can have multiple processes. For example, the Chrome browser creates a different process for every single tab.

A process means a program is in execution. When a program is loaded into the memory and becomes active, the program becomes a process. The process requires some essential resources such as registers and stack. A process can have 1 or more threads. 1 proces moze pitati OS da pokrene 2. proces. If 2 processes need to talk, they can do so by using Inter Process Communication (IPC). Many applications are designed to work this way so that if a worker process get unresponsive, it can be restarted without stopping other processes which are running different parts of the application.

A thread is the smallest unit of execution within a process.



Main differences between process and thread:

  1. Processes are usually independent, while threads exist as subsets of a process.
  2. Each process has its own memory space. Threads that belong to the same process share the same memory.

    3. Upravo to sto svaki proces ima svoj privatni deo memorije nam obezbedjuje sandboxing izmedju task-ova koji se izvrsavaju na razlicitim procesima, a to pak doprinosi i security-u, jer task u 1 procesu ne moze uticati tek tako na 2. task u 2. procesu. <- STA ZAPRAVO ZNACI SANDBOXING.

  3. A process is a heavyweight operation. It takes more time to create and terminate.
  4. Context switching is more expensive between processes.
  5. Inter Thread Communication (ITC) is faster then Inter Process Communication (IPC).

Opsti naziv za servis koji omogucava istu stvar kao i REST samo u suprotnom smeru. Drugim recima, REST omogucava komunikaciju izmedju klijenta i servera pri cemu je klijent inicijator, odnosno klijent salje request. Sa druge strane, webhook omogucava komunikaciju izmedju klijenta i servera pri cemu je server inicijator request-a. Da bi server mogao da dodje do klijenta, klijent mora imati javno dostupnu adresu.


To set up a webhook, the client gives a unique URL to the server API and specifies which event it wants to know about. Once the webhook is set up, the client no longer needs to poll the server; the server will automatically send the relevant payload to the client’s webhook URL when the specified event occurs.

Webhooks are often referred to as reverse APIs or push APIs, because they put the responsibility of communication on the server, rather than the client. Instead of the client sending HTTP requests—asking for data until the server responds—the server sends the client a single HTTP POST request as soon as the data is available. Despite their nicknames, webhooks are not APIs; they work together. An application must have an API to use a webhook. <- GLAVNA CAKA.

xh1 Decorator


A decorator is a Java class that is annotated javax.decorator.Decorator and that has a corresponding decorators element in the beans.xml file. A decorator bean class must also have a delegate injection point, which is annotated javax.decorator.Delegate. This injection point can be a field, a constructor parameter, or an initializer method parameter of the decorator class. Decorators are outwardly (spolja) similar to interceptors. However, they actually perform tasks complementary to those performed by interceptors. Interceptors perform cross-cutting tasks associated with method invocation and with the lifecycles of beans, but cannot perform any business logic. Decorators, on the other hand, do perform business logic by intercepting business methods of beans. This means that instead of being reusable for different kinds of applications, as are interceptors, their logic is specific to a particular application. If an application has both interceptors and decorators, the interceptors are invoked first. This means, in effect, that you cannot intercept a decorator.

xh1 Serialization (serializacija) objekta


Serializable interface is meant solely to identify that the object will be serialized. It doesn't have any methods which means that the serialization process is managed automatically by the runtime system and requires little user-defined code to be written.


Serijalizovana verzija objekta zahteva 3 informacije:

  1. state (polja i njihove vrednosti) objekta,
  2. ime klase i
  3. verziju klase (kako bi bili sigurni da svi koriste istu verziju klase. Ostvaruje se kroz private static final long serialVersionUID polje). Ako se verzija ne definise, Java ce sama izracunati verziju klase pri kompajliranju i cuvati ovu vrednost u .class file-u. Algoritam je definisan specifikacijom i radi na sledeci nacin:

serialVersionUID polje je karakteristicno samo za serializable objekte. Objekti koji ne spadaju u ovu grupu ga nemaju.


Pri deserijalizaciji radi se sledece:

  1. cita se serijalizovani objekat,
  2. nadje se ime klase serijalizovanog objekta i ucita se Class objekat za tu klasu. Ako Class objekat ne moze da se ucita, iz bilo kog razloga, ili ako se ne poklapaju verzije serijalizovanog objekta i Class objekta, deserijalizacija ce fail-ovati.
  3. Rezervise se prostor na heap-u i restore-uje se stanje objekta na heap-u na osnovu serijalizovanog objekta. Napomena je da se ne pokrece konstruktor objekta vec se objekat restore-uje. Ako se pri restore-ovanju objekta naidje na non-serializable ancestor (super-class) onda ce se pokrenuti konstruktor tog ancestor-a kao i svih njegovih super-class-a bez obzira da li su te klase serializable ili ne.

U slucaju da se klasa promeni (polja ili metodi dodati ili izbrisani) ali da verzija klase ostane ista, then the receiving virtual machine will accept to deserialize the object. In this case the default behavior is defined in the deserialization process. If there is no information in the transmitted state to correctly set up a field, then this field will have a default value and if they are undefined field in the serialized object that do not exist anymore in the receiving class then this information will be lost.

xh1 Comparable


compareTo() je u istom rangu kao i equals() i hashCode() metodi. Kada se override-uje moraju se postovati odredjena pravila:

  1. compareTo() must be in consistent with equals() method e.g. if two objects are equal via equals(), there compareTo() must return 0 -> (x.compareTo(y) == 0) == (x.equals(y)). Ako ovo nije ispunjeno, potrebno je to posebno naglasiti: Note: This class has a natural ordering that is inconsistent with equals. A class whose compareTo() method imposes an order that is inconsistent with equals() will still work, but sorted collections containing elements of the class may not obey the general contract of the appropriate collection interfaces (Collection, Set, or Map). This is because the general contracts for these interfaces are defined in terms of the equals() method, but sorted collections use the equality test imposed by compareTo() in place of equals(). It is not a catastrophe if this happens, but it’s something to be aware of.

    2. For example, consider the BigDecimal class, whose compareTo() method is inconsistent with equals(). If you create a HashSet instance and add new BigDecimal("1.0") and new BigDecimal("1.00"), the set will contain 2 elements because the 2 BigDecimal instances added to the set are unequal when compared using the equals() method, because BigDecimal#equals() mehtod compares both value and scale of 2 BigDecimal instances. If, however, you perform the same procedure using a TreeSet instead of a HashSet, the set will contain only 1 element because the 2 BigDecimal instances are equal when compared using the compareTo() method.

  2. compareTo() must throw NullPointerException if current object get compared to null object as opposed to equals() which return false on such scenario.
  3. Don't use subtraction for comparing integral values because result of subtraction can overflow Integer max/min value. It is safe to use only if difference between the lowest and highest possible field values is less than or equal to Integer.MAX_VALUE ( 2 31 -1). To be safe we should use either Integer.compareTo() or logical operators (< or >) for comparison.
  4. Use Float.compareTo() or Double.compareTo() to compare floating point number since relational operator doesn't obey contract of compareTo() for floating point numbers.
  5. When comparing String using compareTo() consider case. Just like equals() consider case, compareTo() also consider case. If you want to compare regardless of case than use String.compareToIgnoreCase().

xh1 Diamond problem


Consider a class A has foo() method and then B and C derived from A and has there own foo() implementation and now class D derive from B and C using multiple inheritance and if we refer just foo(), from D, compiler will not be able to decide which foo() it should invoke. This is called diamond problem because structure on this inheritance scenario is similar to 4 edge diamond:

A foo()

/ \

B foo() C foo()

\ /

D foo()

Ovo je jedan od razloga zasto Java ne podrzava multiple inheritance for classes.

xh1 Class name vs. file name


Razlog zasto public klase treba da imaju isto ime kao i file u kome se nalaze:

Let's assume we have two public classes Y & Z in a file named Y.java. Now, let's assume a different class X is using Z. Now, when we compile X, the compiler first tries to locate Z.class and if it cannot find it, then it tries to locate Z.java so that it can compile it automatically. But, since we only have Y.java, the class Z cannot be located and hence we get a compilation error. So, we do need to place them in separate files.

# Terminologija
## Paket (package)
One of the features of object-oriented languages that makes them powerful is that they are often accompanied by ==class libraries== (biblioteke klasa). These libraries typically contain many hundreds or thousands of different classes that have proved useful to developers on a wide range of different projects. Java calls its libraries ==packages==. Packages can be nested (that is, packages can contain other packages).

Na paket treba gledati kao na ==skup klasa koje pripadaju istoj familiji==.

Library classes are used in exactly the same way as we would use our own classes.

==Naming conventions== when package name has 2 or more words:
- do not use hypen`"-"`(bad practice),
- do not use capital letters inside package names (bad practice),
- just concatenate strings like `com.vujo.mypackage`(prefered),
- use underscore`"_".`
Legalizing package names:
Domain Name / Package Name
- `hyphenated-name.example.org / org.example.hyphenated_name`
- `example.int / int_.example`
- `123name.example.com / com.example._123name`
*[https://docs.oracle.com/javase/tutorial/java/package/namingpkgs.html](https://docs.oracle.com/javase/tutorial/java/package/namingpkgs.html)*

Logicka celina koja reprezentuje nesto, npr. dokument, string, file, … Objekat nudi i mogucnost manipulacije onoga sto reprezentuje koristeci metode koje su prikladne za taj objekat.

letters,
digits,
underscores (`_`) and
dollar signs (`$`)
that does not begin with a digit and does not contain spaces. ==Identifier== moze biti ==ime klase, promenljive ili metoda==.
As of ==Java 9==, you can ==no longer use an underscore (==`_`==) by itself== as an identifier. Notice that you can still use underscores in variable names. The only thing you cannot do is using a single underscore as an identifier.

`naziv paketa + naziv klase`

## Literal
A literal is the ==source code representation of a fixed value==; literals are represented directly in your code without requiring computation. As shown below, it's possible to assign a literal to a variable of a primitive type.

Naziv za ==row data==:
`int x = 10;`
`String name = "John";`
`long y = 10L`
`long y = 10``l```<mark style="background: aqua;">(malo slovo L)</mark>

A floating-point literal is of type `float`if it ends with the letter`F`or`f`; otherwise its type is `double`and it can optionally end with the letter`D`or`d`.
The `10`and`"John"`are just a values itself, called a ==literal values==, because they ==stands alone without being stored in a variable==.
## Expression
==Statements== are made up of one or more expressions.
> [!vazno] Important
> An ==expression== is any reference to a variable or value, or a set of variable(s) and value(s) combined with operators.`<-`OSNOVE!

For example:
`a = b * 2;`
This statement has four expressions in it:
• `2` is a ==literal value expression==.
• `b` is a ==variable expression==, which means to retrieve its current value.
• `b * 2` is an ==arithmetic expression==, which means to do the multiplication.
• `a = b * 2` is an ==assignment expression==, which means to assign the result of the `b * 2` expression to the variable `a.`

> [!vazno] Important
> Statement sama po sebi ne vraca vrednost dok expression vraca.`<-`==SUSTINA!==

Npr. `if/else`petlja je statement sto znaci da`if/else`petlja radi nesto, ali sama po sebi ne vraca vrednost.
## Compiler
Computers, internally, work with a ==binary representation of a machine code (bytecode)==, which looks quite different from Java. The ==compiler== is program whose job is to ==translates the Java code into machine code (bytecode)==.
## Stereotype
Term for an annotation that encapsulates other annotations. Npr. `@Model`je built-in stereotype. Mogu se koristiti da bi nad klasom definisali vise interceptor-a jednom anotacijom.
## Enterprise application
Provide the business logic for an enterprise.
## Business logic
Code that fulfills the purpose of the application.
## Managed Beans
Lightweight container-managed objects (POJOs) with minimal requirements, support a small set of basic services, such as resource injection, lifecycle callbacks, and interceptors. ==Container-managed objects== are:
servlets,
enterprise bean, or
managed bean.
A Java EE ==component is a bean== if the lifecycle of its instances may be managed by the container according to the lifecycle context model defined in the CDI specification.
## Callback methods
User defined methods that are attached to some object's lifecycle events and are invoked automatically by container when these events occur.
## Resource
Program object that ==provides connections to== such ==systems== as database servers and messaging systems.
Each ==resource object== is identified by a unique, people-friendly name, called the ==JNDI name==. For example, the JNDI name of the preconfigured JDBC resource for the Java DB database that is shipped with GlassFish Server is `java:comp/DefaultDataSource`. Applications then use annotations to inject the resources. You inject resources by using the `@Resource` annotation in an application.
## Resource adapter
Software component that allows Java EE application components to access and interact with the underlying resource manager of the EIS. Because a resource adapter is specific to its resource manager, a different resource adapter typically exists for each type of database or enterprise information system (EIS).
## Web module
Smallest deployable and usable unit of web resources. A web module contains web components and static web content files, such as images, which are called ==web resources==. Web module corresponds to a ==web application==.
## Object state
The state of an object consists of the ==values of its instance variables==.
> [!note]
> Generalno govoreci vrednosti koje se nalaze u promenljivama predstavljaju state bilo to objekta, programa ili neceg drugog u cemu su te promenljive definisane.

## Helper class / Utility class
U Java-i je treba koristiti "utility class" naziv za ovakve klase.

Klase sa reusable `static`==metodama==.

Nuzno zlo u Java-i, posto ne postoji nacin da se reuse-uju funkcije van klase.
Pri kreiranju utility klasa treba paziti na barem 2 stvari:
- da li je u pitanju stvarno re-usable funkcionalnost, u suprotnom je treba pripojiti postojećoj klasi koja se bavi baš time i koristiti instance metode a ne `static`.
- ne stavljati sve u jednu helper klasu, već praviti više helper klasi po separation of concerns i package principima. Ako je struktura organizovana na osnovu domena, onda videti da li klasa pripada jednom domenu i staviti je tu, ako ne prebaciti i `shared`folder koji se bavi svim i svačim.
## Coarse-grained object
Contains more data than a fine-grained one.
## Batch (serija, gomila) jobs
Tasks that can be executed without user interaction. These tasks are executed periodically or when resource usage is low, and they often process large amounts of information such as log files, database records, or images. Examples include billing, report generation, data format conversion, and image processing.
The batch framework consists of a job specification language based on XML, a set of batch annotations and interfaces for application classes that implement the business logic, a batch container that manages the execution of batch jobs, and supporting classes and interfaces to interact with the batch container.
## Batch processing
Refers to running batch jobs on a computer system.

==Contains both the compiler and the launcher.==
## Serialize
This means to convert object state to a byte stream so that the byte stream can be reverted back into a copy of the object. Deserialization is the process of converting the serialized form of an object back into a copy of the object.
## Compiling
Process of translating source code (the human-readable description of a program written by a programmer) into the native language of the computer’s processor. It is done with program called a ==compiler==. Na ovaj nacin se build-uju programi.
## Interoperability
Way to enable disparate (razlicit) software applications to work together.

Casting a floating-point value to an integer value may introduce truncation errors (loss of the fractional part) into the result.

## Inheritance
==Conflict resolution strategies==
Rules for how implementation inheritance work:
==Classes win over interfaces.==  If a class in the superclass chain has a declaration for the method (concrete or abstract), you're done, and defaults (from interface) are irrelevant.
==More specific interfaces win over less specific ones== (where specificity means "subtyping").  A default from `List` wins over a default from `Collection`, regardless of where or how or how many times `List` and `Collection` enter the inheritance graph.
If there is not a unique winner according to the above rules, concrete classes must disambiguate manually.
Ovo su pravila koja uvek treba da imamo na umu kada zakljucujemo koji metod ce se naslediti u odredjenom slucaju (kada klasa implementira interface sa npr. `default-nim`metodama i ekstenduje (apstraktnu) klasu).`<-`<mark style="background: red;">VAZNO.</mark>

Par napomena vezaih za `default-ne`metode u interface-u:
- `default-ni`metodi ne mogu override-ovati metode iz `Object`klase.
- kada se eksplicitno poziva `default-ni`metod iz interface-a, koristi se `super`keyword u obliku: `InterfaceName.super.defaultMethodName()``;`. Moze se pozivati samo interface koji klasa direktno implementira, ne i grandparent interface.
*Primer koda Examples/defaultMethods u Test environment folderu.*
- ne mogu se koristiti `final` i `synchronized` keywords uz `default-ne` metode.

> [!note]
> We can inherit`static`methods.

> [!note]
> We call the declared type of the variable the ==static type==, because it is declared in the source code—the static representation of the program.

> [!note]
> We call the type of the object stored in a variable the ==dynamic type==, because it depends on assignments at runtime—the dynamic behavior of the program.

`Vehicle v1 = new Car();`
The static type of`v1`is`Vehicle,`the dynamic type of`v1`is`Car`.
For type checking, the static type is used. The dynamic type is often only known at runtime, so the compiler has no other choice but to use the static type if it wants to do any checks at compile time.
## Compile-time (compiletime) dependency
> [!vazno] Important
> Compiletime dependencies are only the dependencies (other classes) which you use ==DIRECTLY== in the class you're compiling.

Compiletime dependencies are dependencies that we need on our `CLASSPATH`in order to compile artifact.

> [!note]
> Java doesn't actually link anything at compile time. It only verifies the syntax using the matching classes it finds in the `CLASSPATH`. It's not until runtime that everything gets put together and executed based on the `CLASSPATH` at that time.

## Runtime dependency
> [!vazno] Important
> Runtime dependencies covers both the== DIRECT and INDIRECT== dependencies of the class you're running.

Runtime dependencies are dependencies that we need on our `CLASSPATH`in order to run artifact.
Thus, runtime dependencies includes dependencies of dependencies and any reflection dependencies like classnames which you have in a `String,`but are used in `Class#forName()`.￼
> [!note]
> Runtime dependency is more related with the [actual flow-of-control](onenote:OOP.one#OO%20concepts&section-id={9EAA0122-D3CA-4CFE-84D0-8B0771B4DB56}&page-id={BD124AF7-14EC-47DA-9648-C19851B58DC5}&object-id={AE01C8C3-BD8A-0BF1-0C45-5D5EFEC96240}&71&base-path=https://d.docs.live.net/9f971ac3eb8340c4/Documents/OneNote%202016/Programiranje).

## Polymorphism ("many shapes")
Property of an object to take on many different forms.
To put this more precisely, a Java object may be accessed using a reference with the same type as the object, a reference that is a superclass of the object, or a reference that defines an interface the object implements, either directly or through a superclass. So ==reference type and actual object type can be different==. This is why variables holding object types in Java are ==polymorphic variable==. Furthermore, a cast is not required if the object is being reassigned to a super type or interface of the object.
> [!vazno] Important
> So, a ==variable can hold objects of its declared type or of any subtype of its declared type==. This principle is known as ==substitution==`-`we can substitute a subclass object where a superclass object is expected, because the subclass object is a special case of the superclass.

==Superclass== se cesto naziva i ==supertype== ili ==base class== dok se ==subclass== cesto naziva ==subtype== ili ==derived class==.

Kompajler koristi== reference type== da bi odredio ==koji metodi se mogu pozivati== nad objektom dok sa 2. strane ==JVM== koristi ==object type== da bi odlucio ==koji tacno metod da pozove==. JVM ce uvek pozvati najspecificniju verziju metoda, pocevsi od object type-a i iduci ka parent objektima:`<-`<mark style="background: red;">OBRATI PAZNJU. MNOGO VAZNO.</mark>

==Method binding (method lookup or method dispatch)==
Rekli smo da kompajler koristi reference type da bi odredio koji metodi mogu da se pozovu nad odredjenom referencom, dok JVM koristi object type da bi odredio koju implementaciju tog metoda treba pozvati. Ovaj proces se zove method binding. Pored instance metoda, method binding se vrsi i za staticke metode, tj. za svaki metod. ==Definicija== method binding-a je: povezivanje method call-a sa ==odgovarajucom deklaracijom metoda== kao i sa ==odgovarajucom implementacijom== te deklaracije. Method binding se deli na 2 dela:
==method signature binding== i
==method implementation binding==.
Method signature binding se sastoji od sledeceg:
Kada kompajler naidje na method invocation statement, on proverava da li ==tip reference==, nad kojom se metod poziva, ==sadrzi kompatibilan metod==, tj. da li postoji metod koji ima iste ili kompatibilne parametre.
Ako kompajler pronadje takav metod onda on upisuje ==signature tog metoda u bytecode==. Ako ne nadje, onda dobijemo compiler error. Ova logika se primenjuje na sve metode (i instance i `static`). Znaci u svakom slucaju se signature metoda upisuje u bytecode tokom kompajliranja (malo redundantna recenica jer naravno da se upisuje tokom kompajliranja, ne postoji 2. trenutak kad bi nesto moglo da se upise u bytecode ako ne tokom kompajliranja).
Nakon ovoga treba doneti odluku koju ==konkretnu implementaciju metoda== treba izvrsiti tokom runtime-a. Ovu odluku moze doneti ili compiler ili JVM zavisno od tipa metoda. Ako je u pitanju `static`metod onda ==odluku donosi kompajler== na osnovu tipa reference dok u slucaju ==instance== metoda ==odluku donosi JVM== na osnovu tipa objekta. JVM polazi od objekta trazeci 1. metod koji zadovaljava signature iz bytecode-a. Posto se odluka o tome koji staticki metod ce se izvrsti donosi tokom kompajliranja ovo se zove ==compile-time ili early binding==. Sa 2. strane, u slucaju instance metoda imamo ==runtime ili late binding (dynamic binding)==. Late binding je karakteristican za override-ovane metode.
U slucaju ==polja objekta==, primenjuje se ==early binding== na osnovu tipa reference.

when this statement executes, the `display()`method is invoked in the following steps:

## Program vs. process vs. thread
A ==program== is an executable file containing a set of instructions and passively stored on disk. One program can have multiple processes. For example, the Chrome browser creates a different process for every single tab.
A ==process== means a program is in execution. When a program is loaded into the memory and becomes active, the program becomes a process. The process requires some essential resources such as registers and stack. A process can have 1 or more threads. 1 proces moze pitati OS da pokrene 2. proces. If 2 processes need to talk, they can do so by using Inter Process Communication (IPC). Many applications are designed to work this way so that if a worker process get unresponsive, it can be restarted without stopping other processes which are running different parts of the application.
A ==thread== is the smallest unit of execution within a process.

![[img_0-7abec7a03f.png|550]]

Main differences between process and thread:
Processes are usually independent, while threads exist as subsets of a process.
Each process has its own memory space. Threads that belong to the same process share the same memory.
> [!vazno] Important
> Upravo to sto svaki proces ima svoj privatni deo memorije nam obezbedjuje sandboxing izmedju task-ova koji se izvrsavaju na razlicitim procesima, a to pak doprinosi i security-u, jer task u 1 procesu ne moze uticati tek tako na 2. task u 2. procesu.`<-`STA ZAPRAVO ZNACI SANDBOXING.

A process is a heavyweight operation. It takes more time to create and terminate.
Context switching is more expensive between processes.
Inter Thread Communication (ITC) is faster then Inter Process Communication (IPC).

# Diamond problem

Consider a class `A`has `foo()`method and then `B`and `C`derived from `A`and has there own `foo()`implementation and now class `D`derive from `B`and `C`using multiple inheritance and if we refer just `foo(),`from`D,`compiler will not be able to decide which `foo()`it should invoke. This is called diamond problem because structure on this inheritance scenario is similar to 4 edge diamond:
A foo()
/   \
B foo()   C foo()
\   /
D foo()
Ovo je jedan od razloga zasto Java ==ne podrzava multiple inheritance for classes==.

# `Comparable`

`compareTo()` je u istom rangu kao i `equals()` i `hashCode()` metodi. Kada se override-uje moraju se postovati odredjena pravila:
`compareTo()`must be in consistent with `equals()`method e.g. if two objects are equal via `equals(),`there `compareTo()`must return 0`-> (x.compareTo(y) == 0) == (x.equals(y)).`Ako ovo nije ispunjeno, potrebno je to posebno naglasiti: `Note: This class has a natural ordering that is inconsistent with equals.`A class whose `compareTo()`method imposes an order that is inconsistent with `equals()`will still work, but sorted collections containing elements of the class may not obey the general contract of the appropriate collection interfaces (`Collection, Set,`or`Map`). This is because the general contracts for these interfaces are defined in terms of the `equals()`method, but sorted collections use the equality test imposed by `compareTo()`in place of `equals()`. It is not a catastrophe if this happens, but it’s something to be aware of.
> [!tip]
> For example, consider the `BigDecimal`class, whose `compareTo()`method is inconsistent with `equals()`. If you create a `HashSet` instance and add new `BigDecimal("1.0")` and `new BigDecimal("1.00")`, the set will contain 2 elements because the 2 `BigDecimal` instances added to the set are unequal when compared using the `equals()`method, because `BigDecimal#equals()`mehtod compares both value and scale of 2 `BigDecimal`instances. If, however, you perform the same procedure using a `TreeSet`instead of a `HashSet`, the set will contain only 1 element because the 2 `BigDecimal`instances are equal when compared using the `compareTo()`method.

`compareTo()`must throw `NullPointerException`if current object get compared to `null`object as opposed to `equals()`which return `false`on such scenario.
Don't use subtraction for comparing integral values because result of subtraction can overflow `Integer`max/min value. It is safe to use only if difference between the lowest and highest possible field values is less than or equal to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` (				 					 						2 						 							31 						 					 				 -1). To be safe we should use either 				`Integer.compareTo()`or logical operators (< or >) for comparison.
Use `Float.compareTo()`or `Double.compareTo()`to compare floating point number since relational operator doesn't obey contract of `compareTo()`for floating point numbers.
When comparing `String`using `compareTo()`consider case. Just like `equals()```consider case, `compareTo()```also ==consider case==. If you want to compare regardless of case than use `String.compareToIgnoreCase().`

# Serialization (serializacija) objekta

`Serializable`interface is meant solely ==to identify that the object will be serialized==. It doesn't have any methods which means that the serialization process is managed automatically by the runtime system and requires little user-defined code to be written.

Serijalizovana verzija objekta zahteva 3 informacije:
state (polja i njihove vrednosti) objekta,
ime klase i
verziju klase (kako bi bili sigurni da svi koriste istu verziju klase. Ostvaruje se kroz `private static final long serialVersionUID`polje). Ako se verzija ne definise, Java ce sama izracunati verziju klase pri kompajliranju i cuvati ovu vrednost u `.class` file-u. Algoritam je definisan specifikacijom i radi na sledeci nacin:
![[img_0-03cd4acdb4.png|359]]

`serialVersionUID`polje je karakteristicno samo za serializable objekte. Objekti koji ne spadaju u ovu grupu ga nemaju.

Pri deserijalizaciji radi se sledece:
cita se serijalizovani objekat,
nadje se ime klase serijalizovanog objekta i ucita se `Class`objekat za tu klasu. Ako `Class`objekat ne moze da se ucita, iz bilo kog razloga, ili ako se ne poklapaju verzije serijalizovanog objekta i `Class`objekta, deserijalizacija ce fail-ovati.
Rezervise se prostor na heap-u i restore-uje se stanje objekta na heap-u na osnovu serijalizovanog objekta. Napomena je da se ne pokrece konstruktor objekta vec se objekat restore-uje. Ako se pri restore-ovanju objekta naidje na non-serializable ancestor (super-class) onda ce se pokrenuti konstruktor tog ancestor-a kao i svih njegovih super-class-a bez obzira da li su te klase serializable ili ne.

U slucaju da se klasa promeni (polja ili metodi dodati ili izbrisani) ali da verzija klase ostane ista, then the receiving virtual machine will accept to deserialize the object. In this case the default behavior is defined in the deserialization process. If there is no information in the transmitted state to correctly set up a field, then this field will have a default value and if they are undefined field in the serialized object that do not exist anymore in the receiving class then this information will be lost.
Da bi videli vrednost `serialVersionUID-a`, koristimo tool `serialver`:
`serialver com.mds.SomeClass`

Jos 1, prethodna 2 slajda vaze samo ako smo hard-code-ovali `serialVersionUID`u okviru klase, ako nismo onda ce se koristiti default-ni algoritam za izracunavanje `serialVersionUID-a`. If a class is serializable, then it subclass is also serializable. Ali ako class-a nije serializable onda njegovu subclass-u mozemo uciniti serializable tako sto cemo implementirati interface `Serializable`u toj subclass-i.

Kao sto vidimo, ==pri serializaciji== Java objekta, the ==methods are not explicitly stored==. Reason for this is that ==Java code had to be at both ends of the serialization - deserialization "pipe."== In actuality, the class definition, that you are using, have to be on both ends of the "pipe". Dakle program koji radi serializaciju kao i program koji radi deserializaciju, moraju imati pristup klasi objekta koji je serializovan da bi on mogao da se deserializuju.

# `Void`klasa

The `Void`class exists because of the fact that some time we may need ==to represent the ==`void`==keyword as an object==. But at the same point we ==cannot create an instance of the ==`Void`class using the `new`operator. This is because the constructor in `Void`has been declared as `private`. Moreover the `Void`class is a `final`class which means that there is no way we can inherit this class. The only purpose that remains for the existence of the `Void`class is reflection, where ==we can get the return type of a method as ==`void`.
`public class VoidTest{`
`public static void main(String[] args) throws SecurityException, NoSuchMethodException {`
`Class testClass = Test.class.getMethod("someTest",null).getReturnType();`
`System.out.println(testClass == Void.TYPE);`
`System.out.println(testClass == Void.class);`
`}`
`}`
`class Test{`
`public void someTest(){}`
`}`

You can also use `Void`class ==in ==`Generics`==to specify that you don't care about the specific type of object being used==. For example:
`List<void> someList;`

# Paketska struktura u projektu

==Package name== je deo klase i ne moze se menjati a da se ne uradi i rekompajliranje klase. File-ovi koji pripadaju ==istom paketu== mogu biti ==u razlicitim folderima==. U tom slucaju, package name je isti za svaku klasu u paketu ali putanje do klasa mogu pocinjati u razlicitim folderima. Akcenat je na delu "==pocinjati u razlicitim folderima==", odnosno klase mogu pripadati paketu `a.b`i biti u `dir1, dir2`ili`dir3`ali u okviru ovih foldera ce se nalaziti `a/b`folderska struktura. Primer ovoga su testovi u Maven Java Archetype folderskoj strukturi gde testovi pripadaju istom paketu kao i klase koje se testiraju ali se nalaze u razlicitim folderima:
`src/main/java/com/companyName/graphics`
`src/test/java/com/companyName/graphics`
Na ovaj nacin se source logika razdvaja, a sa 2. strane i dalje mozemo pristupati package private (default) metodama i poljima klase koju testiramo.

# Decorator

A ==decorator== is a Java class that is annotated `javax.decorator.Decorator` and that has a corresponding `decorators` element in the `beans.xml` file. A decorator bean class must also have a delegate injection point, which is annotated`javax.decorator.Delegate`. This injection point can be a field, a constructor parameter, or an initializer method parameter of the decorator class. Decorators are outwardly (spolja) similar to interceptors. However, they actually perform tasks complementary to those performed by interceptors. Interceptors perform cross-cutting tasks associated with method invocation and with the lifecycles of beans, but cannot perform any business logic. Decorators, on the other hand, do ==perform business logic by intercepting business methods of beans==. This means that instead of being reusable for different kinds of applications, as are interceptors, their logic is specific to a particular application. If an application has both interceptors and decorators, the interceptors are invoked first. This means, in effect, that you cannot intercept a decorator.
*"25.7 Using Decorators in CDI Applications", JEE 7 Tutorial u Java EE folderu.*

# Webhooks

Opsti naziv za servis koji omogucava istu stvar kao i ==REST== samo ==u suprotnom smeru==. Drugim recima, REST omogucava komunikaciju izmedju klijenta i servera pri cemu je klijent inicijator, odnosno klijent salje request. Sa druge strane, webhook omogucava komunikaciju izmedju klijenta i servera pri cemu je server inicijator request-a. Da bi server mogao da dodje do klijenta, klijent mora imati javno dostupnu adresu.

To set up a webhook, the client gives a unique URL to the server API and specifies which event it wants to know about. Once the webhook is set up, the client no longer needs to poll the server; the server will automatically send the relevant payload to the client’s webhook URL when the specified event occurs.
Webhooks are often referred to as ==reverse APIs or push APIs==, because they put the responsibility of communication on the server, rather than the client. Instead of the client sending HTTP requests—asking for data until the server responds—the server sends the client a single HTTP `POST`request as soon as the data is available. Despite their nicknames, webhooks are not APIs; they work together. ==An application must have an API to use a webhook.==`<-`==GLAVNA CAKA.==

# `java.util.Random`

Supports generation of pseudo-random values—typically, random numbers. The sequence of numbers generated is determined by a seed value, which may be passed to a constructor or set via a call to `setSeed()`.
> [!tip]
> Two `Random`objects starting from the same seed will return the same sequence of values to identical calls.
